rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Columbia Mechanical Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for relief from default is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEANNE ROBERTS et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Pnuemo Abex LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1(causation) except for the third cause of action for products liability for clutch components and brake assemblies mechanisms and linings which plaintiffs say they will dismiss and denied as to issue 2 (punitive damages). The deposition testimony of Mr. Nelson, Mr. LeCour and Mr. Allen, the declarations of Mr. Ewing and Dr. Horn, and defendant's responses to GO 129 interrogatories create triable issues whether Mr. Pera was exposed to asbestos from a brake product attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Pera's mesothelioma. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oprression by clear and convincing evidence. Plaintiffs' responses to contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's objections to the deposition testiomony of Mr. Nelson, Mr. LeCour and Mr. Allen, the declarations of Mr. Ewing and Dr. Horn and its own GO 129 interrogatory responses are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax And Strike Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.S Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax and strike defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant¿s request for attorneys¿ fees has been the subject of another motion. This motion only concerns costs other than those listed on line 13 of defendant¿s costs memorandum. Defendant withdraws its requests for $29.56 in travel costs for the Boeing PMK deposition and $59.48 for the deposition of Christopher Gauger and thus the motion is granted as to those costs. The motion is denied as to all other costs including the ¿travel¿ costs for the depositions of Margaret Gauger and Charles Gauger (incorrectly listed as travel costs for Margaret Gauger). Phone conferencing charges for the depositions of Margaret Guager and Charles Gauger are recoverable and were reasonably necessary for the conduct of the litigation. = (220/HEK)
